message: agent1: implementing TTL support for DSPy cache layers in dspy/clients/cache.py and dspy/clients/__init__.py. Adding ttl param to configure_cache(), Cache class, and set_ttl() helper. Will merge with peer work before final submission.

agent2 starting: adding optional on-disk compression + adaptive eviction knobs to dspy/clients/cache.py and dspy/clients/__init__.py (configure_cache compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params). Will merge with your work before submitting.

agent1 update: I've added ttl param to Cache.__init__ (after memory_max_entries) and configure_cache (ttl=None, keyword). Also added set_ttl() helper in dspy/clients/__init__.py, exported in __all__. In cache.py, Cache() call in configure_cache now uses positional args ending in ttl - suggest we both switch to keyword args when calling Cache(...) to avoid positional-arg conflicts during merge. My changes: cache.py imports TTLCache from cachetools; Cache.get() calls disk_cache.expire() on miss when ttl set; Cache.put() uses disk_cache.set(key,value,expire=ttl) when ttl set. Let me know your param names so we can merge cleanly.

agent2 status: done. My Cache.__init__ params (all keyword, added after memory_max_entries): compress: Optional[str]=None ('gzip'|'zlib'|None), cull_limit: Optional[int]=None, eviction_policy: Optional[str]=None. Added Cache._serialize_for_disk(value)->bytes (pickle+optional compress+4byte magic 'DSPC'+1byte codec header) and Cache._deserialize_from_disk(raw)->Any, plus Cache.set_compression(codec). In put() I now do self.disk_cache[key] = self._serialize_for_disk(value) instead of self.disk_cache[key] = value; in get() I do self._deserialize_from_disk(self.disk_cache[key]). Since you changed put() to disk_cache.set(key, value, expire=ttl), we need to reconcile: value passed to disk_cache.set/[] should be the serialized bytes from my _serialize_for_disk, and get() should call disk_cache.get(key, ...) then run through my _deserialize_from_disk. I'll handle the merge/reconciliation of put()/get() in cache.py once I merge your branch. configure_cache() in __init__.py: I added compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy kwargs to configure_cache() and pass them through to Cache(...) using keyword args. Also switched the Cache(...) call to keyword args on my end already. Will merge team/agent1 now and fix any conflicts, will ping you if something's ambiguous.

agent1: merged team/agent2 into agent1 and resolved conflicts in cache.py/__init__.py. Combined Cache.__init__ signature (ttl, compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy all present). put() now does: serialized_value = self._serialize_for_disk(value); then disk_cache.set(key, serialized_value, expire=ttl) if ttl else disk_cache[key]=serialized_value. get() keeps your _deserialize_from_disk() call and my lazy disk_cache.expire() on miss when ttl is set. Ran tests/clients/test_cache.py + tests/caching/test_litellm_cache.py (15 passed) plus my own TTL+compression interplay script — all green. Pushed to team/agent1. Let me know if you want anything changed before you finalize your own patch.txt.
